_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the key features of Australia's democratic system, including how people are represented in government and how elections work.

_source.w: We are learning about the key features of Australia's democratic system, including how people are represented in government and how elections work.
y: Understanding how our democracy works helps us participate in it as informed citizens.
Elections and representation are how everyday people have a say in how the country is run.
Knowing about democratic institutions helps us appreciate and protect the freedoms we have.
eg: Exploring how voters elect representatives to make decisions on their behalf in parliament.
Comparing the House of Representatives and the Senate and their different roles.
Investigating how preferential voting works and why Australia uses it.
Discussing why it is compulsory to vote in Australia and what this means for democracy.
Looking at how electoral boundaries are drawn and why this matters for fair representation.
Simulating a class election using Australian voting procedures.
